close_reason: Implemented in commit 594a894f: Shale.Server now detects emfile/enfile/enomem errors during initialization and retries with exponential backoff (1s-30s) instead of crash-looping

comments.text:
## Problem

When the system runs out of file descriptors (`emfile` error), Shale.Server crashes during initialization when trying to read WAL segment files:

```
** (RuntimeError) Unable to read WAL segment files from path: /var/.../qlfgfjyw. 
Check directory permissions and filesystem health.
```

The GenServer then restarts and immediately crashes again, creating a crash loop that:
1. Consumes supervisor restart budget
2. Makes diagnosis harder (floods logs)
3. Doesn't give the system time to recover file descriptors

## Expected Behavior

- Detect `emfile` errors specifically
- Back off with exponential delay before retrying
- Log a clear message about file descriptor exhaustion
- Consider staying in a degraded/unavailable state rather than crash-looping

## Location

`lib/bedrock/data_plane/log/shale/server.ex:119` - `handle_continue(:initialization)`

## Reproduction

Run Livebook with default macOS file descriptor limits (~256) and start multiple Bedrock clusters or workers until `emfile` errors appear.
comments.author: Jason Allum

comments.text:
## Additional Context

User has `ulimit -n 1048575` (1M+ file descriptors) yet still hit exhaustion. This suggests a file descriptor leak rather than just a low system limit. 

May need a separate investigation bead for the leak itself - this bead focuses on graceful handling when exhaustion occurs.
comments.author: Jason Allum

title: Shale: handle file descriptor exhaustion gracefully instead of crash-looping
